mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in near-term revenue growth.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative forward returns in >55% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls increase exceeding 150,000 confirms robust economic expansion and supports corporate profit expectations, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of months where PAYEMS exceeds the 150k threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings reports validate macro trends.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with accelerating inflation indicates supply constraints and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as markets anticipate central bank tightening.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment alongside steady payroll growth signals a healthy labor market transition that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative in >60% of periods where UNRATE falls and PAYEMS rises simultaneously during recovery phases.
